{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"api_error_status":null,"duration_ms":417781,"duration_api_ms":418040,"ttft_ms":3743,"num_turns":54,"result":"Review complete. Verdict: **PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** — CP00-260 does not block pack or goal closeout.

**What I verified (read-only):**
- All distribution counts reconcile across plan/manifest/baseline/registry (phase 98+52=150; deliverables and micro-titles each sum to 150).
- All 7 required CP260 exports exist, are surfaced via the wildcard `index.js`, tested in `model.test.js`, and deep-equal projected into `contracts/search-core-contract.json`.
- Descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data boundaries enforced in `freezeCp260Result`, the no-write attestation, the descriptor validator, and tests (locks/retry/rollback/hermes-receipt false; deny-over-allow true; leak guards present).
- Claude stays read-only and non-final-approver; Hermes is used only for deterministic descriptor evidence, not product runtime.
- Command evidence: 19 gates green (exit 0); `production_ready` honestly `false` pending review; `closeout-pack:validate` and post-review artifacts correctly deferred.
- Hardening preconditions clean: auth preflight valid JSON, no in-scope invalid attempts (LDIP file is unrelated/preserved).

**Findings:** 0 P0 / 0 P1 / 0 P2 / 1 P3. The single P3 is a non-blocking validator-symmetry note: the targeted failure-row checks in `validators.js:5209` cover `RP07.P07.M01` but not the identical-titled `RP07.P07.M00` rows — harmless because the universal descriptor-only loop already validates every row and the rows are deterministically generated from frozen extras.","stop_reason":"end_turn","session_id":"decf1d6f-ff31-4314-a641-a2238a18205c","total_cost_usd":3.8658077499999997,"usage":{"input_tokens":1366,"cache_creation_input_tokens":123227,"cache_read_input_tokens":4655176,"output_tokens":30399,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":123227,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[{"input_tokens":2,"output_tokens":608,"cache_read_input_tokens":154492,"cache_creation_input_tokens":8264,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":8264},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"},"modelUsage":{"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001":{"inputTokens":1146,"outputTokens":20,"cacheReadInputTokens":0,"cacheCreationInputTokens":0,"webSearchRequests":0,"costUSD":0.0012460000000000001,"contextWindow":200000,"maxOutputTokens":32000},"claude-opus-4-8":{"inputTokens":1366,"outputTokens":30399,"cacheReadInputTokens":4655176,"cacheCreationInputTokens":123227,"webSearchRequests":0,"costUSD":3.8645617499999996,"contextWindow":200000,"maxOutputTokens":32000}},"permission_denials":[],"structured_output":{"overall_verdict":"PASS_WITH_FINDINGS","blocks_pack_closeout":false,"blocks_goal_closeout":false,"no_unresolved_p0":true,"no_unresolved_p1":true,"summary":"CP00-260 is a descriptor-only closeout pack that closes the RP07.P06 permission tail (M06 Synthetic Fixture Set tail through M10 Closeout And Next Handoff) and opens the RP07.P07 failure/recovery foundation (M00-M02 head) as eight descriptor sections over 150 planned RP07 units (RP07.P06.M06.S14-RP07.P07.M02.S12). All distributions reconcile across plan, manifest, baseline, and registry requirements: phase counts (RP07.P06=98 + RP07.P07=52 = 150), deliverable counts (claude_review 6, ui 17, security_audit 28, test 22, implementation 35, hermes_evidence 5, failure_recovery 35, fixture 2 = 150), and micro-title counts (sum 150). All 7 required public exports exist in service.js/validators.js, are surfaced through the wildcard packages/search/src/index.js barrel (unchanged but correct), are imported/asserted in model.test.js, and are deep-equal projected into contracts/search-core-contract.json. No-write/no-real-data/descriptor-only boundaries are enforced consistently in freezeCp260Result (all runtime/write/leak flags false, synthetic_only/no_real_data/deny_over_allow_enforced true), SEARCH_CORE_CP260_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (promotes_claude_to_final_approval false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation false), the descriptor validator, and unit tests. Authority boundary holds: Claude is read-only with tools Read/Grep/Glob and is not the final approver (claude_is_final_approval false; human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening true); enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence/receipt shape (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product false), not product runtime. Command evidence records all 19 implementation/plan/repo-wide gates passing (exit 0), with pack-level production_ready honestly false (status implemented_pending_review) and closeout-pack:validate correctly deferred as a post-review gate; post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are appropriately not yet produced. Review-hardening preconditions are clean: auth preflight passed with well-formed JSON, no in-scope invalid review attempts, and the unrelated LDIP invalid-attempt file is explicitly preserved out of scope. One non-blocking P3 defense-in-depth observation on validator symmetry is noted. No P0/P1/P2 issues; the pack's gating posture and boundaries are sound.","findings":[{"id":"CP260-P3-01","severity":"P3","title":"Failure-section descriptor validator deep-checks RP07.P07.M01 rows but not the identical RP07.P07.M00 rows","file":"packages/search/src/validators.js","line":5209,"details":"In validateSearchCoreCp260P06CloseoutP07FailureFoundationDescriptor, the shared failSection loop (lines 5201-5208) asserts cross_tenant/permission_denied/ambiguous_rule/lock_conflict/retry_exhaustion guards for all three failure micros (RP07.P07.M00, M01, M02). However, the extra row-specific guards for rollback_expectation.performs_rollback_runtime, blocked_claim_receipt.blocked_claim_detail_included, failure_fixture.real_client_data_loaded, failure_integration_smoke.dispatches_integration_smoke_runtime, audit_failure_hint.audit_hint_detail_included, and hermes_failure_evidence.emits_hermes_runtime_receipt (lines 5209-5215) are asserted only against the M01 section (const m01 = sections['RP07.P07.M01']). RP07.P07.M00 contains the same six row titles (per required_section_rows) but is not subjected to these targeted assertions. This is not an exploitable gap: every row in every section (including M00) is still validated descriptor_only===true, runtime_execution===false, customer_safe_errors_only===true, and raw_payload_included===false by the universal loop (lines 5181-5200), and all rows are deterministically generated from the frozen SEARCH_CORE_CP260_ROW_EXTRAS, so no divergent/unsafe value can be present in M00. The validator and full test/command-evidence suite pass (exit 0). It is purely a validator-symmetry / defense-in-depth observation.","recommendation":"For future descriptor packs, extend the M01-specific deep assertions to also cover RP07.P07.M00 (which carries the same rollback/blocked-claim/fixture/smoke/audit-hint/hermes row titles), e.g. by iterating the targeted checks over both M00 and M01. No change is required for CP00-260 closeout, since the universal descriptor-only loop and frozen, deterministic row extras already guarantee the boundary on every M00 row.","post_review_disposition":"non_blocking_defense_in_depth_suggestion; does not block pack or goal closeout; logged for future pack hardening"}],"finding_counts":{"p0":0,"p1":0,"p2":0,"p3":1,"reported_p0":0,"reported_p1":0,"reported_p2":0,"reported_p3":1}},"terminal_reason":"completed","fast_mode_state":"off","uuid":"b843af86-d979-4f76-bbc8-ab2a1193f45e"}
